Infineon
AUTOMOTIVE GRADE
AUIRF2804 AUIRF2804S AUIRF2804L
Features
· Advanced Process Technology
· Ultra Low On-Resistance
· 175℃ Operating Temperature
· Fast Switching
· Repetitive Avalanche Allowed up to Tjmax
· Lead-Free, RoHS Compliant
· Automotive Qualified *

195A
Description
Specifically designed for Automotive applications, this HEXFET® Power MOSFET utilizes the latest processing techniques to achieve extremely low on-resistance per silicon area. Additional features of this design are a 175℃ junction operating temperature, fast switching speed and improved repetitive avalanche rating. These features combine to make this design an extremely efficient and reliable device for use in Automotive applications and wide variety of other applications.

Absolute Maximum Ratings
Stresses beyond those listed under "Absolute Maximum Ratings" may cause permanent damage to the device. These are stress ratings only; and functional operation of the device at these or any other condition beyond those indicated in the specifications is not implied. Exposure to absolute-maximum-rated conditions for extended periods may affect device reliability. The thermal resistance and power dissipation ratings are measured under board mounted and still air conditions. Ambient temperature (TA) is 25℃, unless otherwise specified.

7 This is applied to D2Pak When mounted on 1" square PCB (FR-4 or G-10 Material). For recommended footprint and soldering techniques refer to application note #AN-994
8 Max RDS(on) for D2Pak and TO-262 (SMD) devices.
9 TO-220 device will have an Rth value of 0.45℃/W.
10 All AC and DC test condition based on old Package limitation current = 75A.

Notes on Repetitive Avalanche Curves , Figures 15, 16: (For further info, see AN-1005 at www.infineon.com)
1. Avalanche failures assumption:
Purely a thermal phenomenon and failure occurs at a temperature far in excess of Tjmax. This is validated for every part type.
2. Safe operation in Avalanche is allowed as long as Tjmax is not exceeded.
3. Equation below based on circuit and waveforms shown in Figures 12a, 12b.
4. PD (ave) = Average power dissipation per single avalanche pulse.
5. BV = Rated breakdown voltage (1.3 factor accounts for voltage increase during avalanche).
6. Iav = Allowable avalanche current.
7. AT = Allowable rise in junction temperature, not to exceed Tjmax (assumed as 25°℃ in Figure 15, 16).
tav = Average time in avalanche. D = Duty cycle in avalanche = tav .f
ZthJC(D, tav) = Transient thermal resistance, see Figures 13)
PD (ave) = 1/2 ( 1.3.BV.lav) = AT/ ZthJC lav = 2AT/ [1.3.BV.Zth] EAS (AR) = PD (ave)*tav

lines.content: · Repetitive Avalanche Allowed up to Tjmax
Specifically designed for Automotive applications, this
HEXFET® Power MOSFET utilizes the latest processing
techniques to achieve extremely low on-resistance per silicon
area. Additional features of this design are a 175℃ junction
operating temperature, fast switching speed and improved
repetitive avalanche rating. These features combine to make
this design an extremely efficient and reliable device for use in
Automotive applications and wide variety of other applications.
Stresses beyond those listed under "Absolute Maximum Ratings" may cause permanent damage to the device. These are stress
ratings only; and functional operation of the device at these or any other condition beyond those indicated in the specifications is not
implied. Exposure to absolute-maximum-rated conditions for extended periods may affect device reliability. The thermal resistance
and power dissipation ratings are measured under board mounted and still air conditions. Ambient temperature (TA) is 25℃, unless
Single Pulse Avalanche Energy Tested Value 6
Soldering Temperature, for 10 seconds (1.6mm from case)
HEXFET® is a registered trademark of Infineon.
*Qualification standards can be found at www.infineon.com
Dynamic Electrical Characteristics @ T = 25℃ (unless otherwise specified)
Intrinsic turn-on time is negligible (turn-on is dominated by Ls+LD)
Calculated continuous current based on maximum allowable junction temperature. Bond wire current limit is 195A.
Note that current limitations arising from heating of the device leads may occur with some lead mounting arrangements.
2 Repetitive rating; pulse width limited by max. junction temperature. (See fig. 11)
3 Limited by TJmax, Starting TJ = 25℃, L = 0.24mH, RG = 2522, IAS = 75A, VGs =10V. Part not recommended for use above this value.
5 Coss eff. is a fixed capacitance that gives the same charging time as Coss while Vos is rising from 0 to 80% VDss.
6 This value determined from sample failure population, starting TJ = 25℃, L = 0.24mH, RG = 2522, IAS = 75A, VGs =10V.
7 This is applied to D2Pak When mounted on 1" square PCB (FR-4 or G-10 Material). For recommended footprint and
soldering techniques refer to application note #AN-994
8 Max RDS(on) for D2Pak and TO-262 (SMD) devices.
9 TO-220 device will have an Rth value of 0.45℃/W.
10 All AC and DC test condition based on old Package limitation current = 75A.
Fig 9. Maximum Drain Current vs. Case Temperature
2. Peak Tj = P dm x Zthjc + Tc
Fig 11. Maximum Effective Transient Thermal Impedance, Junction-to-Case
Fig 15. Typical Avalanche Current vs. Pulse width
Notes on Repetitive Avalanche Curves , Figures 15, 16:
(For further info, see AN-1005 at www.infineon.com)
Purely a thermal phenomenon and failure occurs at a temperature far in
excess of Tjmax. This is validated for every part type.
2. Safe operation in Avalanche is allowed as long as Tjmax is not exceeded.
3. Equation below based on circuit and waveforms shown in Figures 12a, 12b.
4. PD (ave) = Average power dissipation per single avalanche pulse.
5. BV = Rated breakdown voltage (1.3 factor accounts for voltage increase
7. AT = Allowable rise in junction temperature, not to exceed Tjmax (assumed as
D = Duty cycle in avalanche = tav .f
ZthJC(D, tav) = Transient thermal resistance, see Figures 13)

paragraphs.content: · Repetitive Avalanche Allowed up to Tjmax

Specifically designed for Automotive applications, this HEXFET® Power MOSFET utilizes the latest processing techniques to achieve extremely low on-resistance per silicon area. Additional features of this design are a 175℃ junction operating temperature, fast switching speed and improved repetitive avalanche rating. These features combine to make this design an extremely efficient and reliable device for use in Automotive applications and wide variety of other applications.

Stresses beyond those listed under "Absolute Maximum Ratings" may cause permanent damage to the device. These are stress ratings only; and functional operation of the device at these or any other condition beyond those indicated in the specifications is not implied. Exposure to absolute-maximum-rated conditions for extended periods may affect device reliability. The thermal resistance and power dissipation ratings are measured under board mounted and still air conditions. Ambient temperature (TA) is 25℃, unless otherwise specified.
Single Pulse Avalanche Energy Tested Value 6

Soldering Temperature, for 10 seconds (1.6mm from case)

Between lead, P 6mm (0.25in.) from package G and center of die contact 5
Intrinsic turn-on time is negligible (turn-on is dominated by Ls+LD)

3 Limited by TJmax, Starting TJ = 25℃, L = 0.24mH, RG = 2522, IAS = 75A, VGs =10V. Part not recommended for use above this value.
4 Pulse width ≤ 1.0ms; duty cycle ≤ 2%. 5 Coss eff. is a fixed capacitance that gives the same charging time as Coss while Vos is rising from 0 to 80% VDss.
6 This value determined from sample failure population, starting TJ = 25℃, L = 0.24mH, RG = 2522, IAS = 75A, VGs =10V.
7 This is applied to D2Pak When mounted on 1" square PCB (FR-4 or G-10 Material). For recommended footprint and soldering techniques refer to application note #AN-994
8 Max RDS(on) for D2Pak and TO-262 (SMD) devices.
